decisions.title: Scale backend-grpc to production or defer Canvas v1 performance promises
decisions.context: ENG-10037/10150 show load testing at 5M pts/sec target. Canvas users expect sub-second query response. Need decision on infrastructure investment vs. managing user expectations for Q2 launch.

arguments.for: Canvas adoption depends on performance. Early enterprise pilots need proof of scale. Infrastructure debt compounds if delayed.
arguments.against: 5M pts/sec may exceed actual Q2 demand. Could ship with 2M capacity, learn usage patterns, scale based on evidence. Preserves eng capacity for Explore V2 features.

decisions.title: Ship family stats in Explore V2 without full chart parity or wait for ENG-6405
decisions.context: ENG-7858 enables dynamic window switching for families. ENG-6405 adds families to all charts. Partial ship means power users get 70% of value 6 weeks earlier but creates support burden explaining gaps.

arguments.for: Ship families in line charts now (ENG-7858). Unblocks 3 design partners doing comparative analysis. Gather evidence on which chart types actually matter before building all.
arguments.against: Incomplete features hurt 'Nail the Core' perception. Users expect feature parity. Better to ship complete families experience in May than partial in April.

decisions.title: Prioritize client-side parsing (ENG-9968/9969/9980) over server-side data-batcher stability
decisions.context: Client-side parsing (HDF5, CH10, TDMS) enables edge deployment vision. Data-batcher coherence (ENG-10149) solves today's ingestion reliability. Both compete for same backend team capacity.
decisions.blockedBy: Need evidence on current data-batcher failure rate vs. customer demand signal for edge parsing. Requesting oncall metrics + customer interview summary by 4/11.

arguments.for: Client-side parsing is moat-building differentiator for Sift Edge. Reduces server costs long-term. Only 2 customers hitting data-batcher issues vs 8 asking about edge.
arguments.against: Data integrity issues erode trust in core platform. Fix stability first, then innovate. Edge parsing won't matter if customers lose confidence in cloud ingestion.

decisions.title: Allow publishing calculated channels from Explore (ENG-7771) in May or hold for Python Rules GA
decisions.context: Users want to share CC's across team without Python Rules complexity. Feature overlaps with Python Rules value prop launching Q3. Risk cannibalizing Python Rules adoption.

arguments.for: Reusable CC's are 80% solution for 90% of users. Accelerates Embed Deeper by making Explore stickier. Python Rules targets different persona (data engineers vs analysts).
arguments.against: Confuses product narrative. Better to hold CC publishing, drive Python Rules adoption, then offer CC's as 'lite' alternative based on usage evidence.

timeAudit.weekSummary: High coordination tax on backend-grpc scaling decision. 6 hours in sync meetings, 4 hours in architecture reviews. Only 8 focus hours this week for strategic thinking.
alerts: 3 decision deadlines in next 9 days - risk of analysis paralysis
Backend team at 110% capacity with load testing + data-batcher + parsing
No 1:1 scheduled this week with Explore V2 PM on family stats decision
